title: cuphea aspera
intro: Cuphea aspera, also known as hairy cuphea or hairy smoke bush, is a shrub-like plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Lythraceae. Traditionally, Cuphea aspera has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Additionally, Cuphea aspera has been used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to soothe respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma.
